category.label: Execution
category.description: Turning a paper signal into real PnL. Spread, slippage, market impact, optimal trade scheduling, and how much to bet on each idea — all the things that turn a 'profitable' backtest into a real strategy.

entries.title: Transaction Costs and Slippage
entries.subtitle: Spread + market impact + opportunity cost — the three components that turn a paper-profitable signal into a real or imaginary strategy. Almgren-Chriss optimal execution.

external_links.title: Almgren & Chriss (2001) — Optimal Execution of Portfolio Transactions

entries.title: Position Sizing (Kelly, Fractional Kelly, Vol Targeting)
entries.subtitle: How much to risk on each trade. The full Kelly criterion, why fractional Kelly is the real-world answer, and volatility-targeting as a more robust alternative.

external_links.title: Kelly (1956) — A New Interpretation of Information Rate
Asness, Frazzini, Pedersen — Leverage Aversion and Risk Parity
